message.content:
Explore the codebase at C:\Users\sergio\gestor-proyectos to understand:
1. The current data model for tasks and subtasks (migrations, models)
2. How SubtaskController works (routes, JSON responses)
3. How the frontend handles subtasks (Alpine.js, Blade templates)
4. Any existing tag/label infrastructure
5. The task modal and card components

Key files to check:
- app/Models/Task.php, app/Models/Subtask.php
- app/Http/Controllers/SubtaskController.php
- database/migrations/ (all migration files)
- resources/views/tasks/_card.blade.php, _modal.blade.php
- resources/views/projects/show.blade.php
- routes/web.php
- resources/js/app.js

Report back: model relationships, DB columns, controller methods, and how Alpine.js manages subtask UI interactions.

content.text: Good. It appears there's no existing tag/label infrastructure. Let me now look at the ProjectController to understand the overall flow:

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me prepare a detailed exploration report. ## Codebase Exploration Report I've thoroughly explored the gestor-proyectos codebase. Here's what I found: ### 1. Current Data Model & Database Schema **Model Hierarchy:** ``` User (one-to-many) → Project (one-to-many) → Task (one-to-many) → Subtask ``` **Database Tables:** - **projects** table: - id, user_id (FK to users), name, description, deadline (nullable), status (enum: active|archived), timestamps - **tasks** table: - id, project_id (FK), title, description (nullable), priority (enum: high|medium|low), status (enum: backlog|in_progress|testing|done), estimated_hours (decimal 8,2, nullable), position (integer for ordering), timestamps - **subtasks** table: - id, task_id (FK), title, is_completed (boolean, default false), estimated_hours (decimal 8,2, nullable), timestamps **Model Relationships:** - `Task.belongsTo(Project)`, `Task.hasMany(Subtask)` - `Subtask.belongsTo(Task)` - `User.hasMany(Project)` - `Project.hasMany(Task)`, `Project.hasMany('tasks.subtasks')` ### 2. SubtaskController Implementation **Location:** `C:\Users\sergio\gestor-proyectos\app\Http\Controllers\SubtaskController.php` All endpoints return JSON (used by Alpine.js via fetch). Methods: - `store(Request $request, Task $task)` — POST /tasks/{task}/subtasks - Validates: title (required, max 255), estimated_hours (nullable, numeric, min 0) - Returns: `{success: true, subtask: {...}}` - Authorization: via TaskPolicy (checks task's project is owned by user) - `update(Request $request, Subtask $subtask)` — PUT /subtasks/{subtask} - Validates: title, estimated_hours (same as above) - Returns: `{success: true, subtask: {...}}` - `toggle(Subtask $subtask)` — PATCH /subtasks/{subtask}/toggle - Toggles is_completed boolean - Returns: `{success: true, is_completed: boolean}` - `destroy(Subtask $subtask)` — DELETE /subtasks/{subtask} - Returns: `{success: true}` **Authorization:** All methods check via SubtaskPolicy (verifies `user.id === subtask.task.project.user_id`) ### 3. Frontend Subtask Handling (Alpine.js) **Location:** `C:\Users\sergio\gestor-proyectos\resources\views\projects\show.blade.php` (lines 119-289) **Alpine.js Component: `kanbanBoard()`** Subtask-specific methods: - `toggleSubtask(subtaskId, checkbox)` — PATCH /subtasks/{id}/toggle, updates DOM optimistically - `deleteSubtask(subtaskId, el)` — DELETE /subtasks/{id} with confirmation dialog - `addSubtask(form)` — POST /tasks/{activeTask.id}/subtasks via FormData - Reads: title, estimated_hours - On success: pushes to `this.activeTask.subtasks` and resets form - Updates modal in real-time **Task Opening:** - `openTask(taskId)` fetches GET /tasks/{id} (returns JSON with loaded subtasks) - Stores in `this.activeTask` and opens task-detail modal ### 4. Blade Template Structure **Task Modal: `C:\Users\sergio\gestor-proyectos\resources\views\tasks\_modal.blade.php` (lines 111-154)** Subtasks section renders: ```blade ``` Form to add subtask (lines 141-153): ```blade
``` **Task Card: `C:\Users\sergio\gestor-proyectos\resources\views\tasks\_card.blade.php` (lines 22-25)** ```blade @php $subtaskCount = $task->subtasks->count(); $doneCount = $task->subtasks->where('is_completed', true)->count(); @endphp @if($subtaskCount > 0) ✓ {{ $doneCount }}/{{ $subtaskCount }} @endif ``` Shows completion progress badge on cards. ### 5. Routing **Location:** `C:\Users\sergio\gestor-proyectos\routes\web.php` (lines 36-40) ```php Route::post('/tasks/{task}/subtasks', [SubtaskController::class, 'store'])->name('subtasks.store'); Route::put('/subtasks/{subtask}', [SubtaskController::class, 'update'])->name('subtasks.update'); Route::delete('/subtasks/{subtask}', [SubtaskController::class, 'destroy'])->name('subtasks.destroy'); Route::patch('/subtasks/{subtask}/toggle', [SubtaskController::class, 'toggle'])->name('subtasks.toggle'); ``` All behind `auth` + `verified` middleware. ### 6. No Existing Tag/Label Infrastructure Searched entire codebase — there are **zero migrations, models, or views** related to tags, labels, or categories. This is a blank slate for adding tag functionality. ### 7. Additional Context - **Authorization Pattern:** All gate checks trace ownership through relationship chain (user → project → tasks → subtasks) - **CSS:** Custom utilities in `resources/css/app.css` for Kanban styling (kanban-scroll, sortable-ghost, line-clamp-2) - **AI Integration:** `AIController.decomposeTask()` analyzes task title/description and suggests subtasks (used when creating new tasks) - **Drag-Drop:** SortableJS handles Kanban card reordering; position field stores order within status columns - **Dark Mode:** Tailwind dark mode enabled (class strategy in tailwind.config.js) --- **Key Architecture Patterns Observed:** - RESTful JSON endpoints for AJAX operations - Alpine.js for reactive UI (no Vue/React) - Optimistic UI updates followed by server confirmation - Clear separation: web routes return HTML views, tasks/subtasks endpoints return JSON - All form data validated server-side - Cascade delete on FK constraints (project → tasks → subtasks) The codebase is well-structured and ready for tag/label feature addition.
